sourceMovie.title: Detective Conan: The Phantom of Baker Street
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Detective Conan: The Phantom of Baker Street (2002) as a animation theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The animation and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: The Phantom of Baker Street clicked for you, Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Batman: Gotham Knight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: The Phantom of Baker Street clicked for you, Batman: Gotham Knight (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and science fiction, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Nancy Drew
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Detective Conan: The Phantom of Baker Street clicked for you, Nancy Drew (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and mystery, with comic timing and sharper banter. faq.question: What should I watch after Detective Conan: The Phantom of Baker Street?
faq.answer: Try Case Closed: The Fourteenth Target, Batman: Gotham Knight, Nancy Drew—each shares DNA with Detective Conan: The Phantom of Baker Street while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Detective Conan: The Phantom of Baker Street?
faq.answer: Detective Conan: The Phantom of Baker Street (2002) is best described as Animation, Adventure, Mystery, Science Fiction, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Detective Conan: The Phantom of Baker Street part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Detective Conan Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
